Abstract—Lawyers perform heavy document drafting, critical
analysis of case files and prepare an argument strategy. The
tasks are time-eating and require proper domain knowledge
and may fall of prey of human error when done manually.
This project provides a unified platform for drafting, document
understanding and argument generation. This

cument
understanding and argument generation. This project facilitates
users in generating structured legal documents like Plaints,
Petitions and Affidavits based on the prompts provided by the
user. It provides a strategic argument generation module which
delivers arguments and the expected counter-arguments. The
system provides a “chat with PDF” option by answering the
queries of the users based on the PDF he or she uploaded.
The backend of the project is built using REST architecture
with JWT

project is built using REST architecture
with JWT-based authentication and MongoDB for data storage,
document history and chat logs. the Gemma 3 (4B) model is
particularly trained using open-source legal datasets comprising
judgements,regulatory documents and case files. PDF parsing
is done using PyMuPDF for text extraction and preprocessing.
evaluation process across legal scenarios (20) depicts structural
completeness, accurate factually and argument correctness with
an expert human rating of

rgument correctness with
an expert human rating of 4.1 out of 5.0.
Index Terms—Plaint, Petition, Affidavit, Document Under-
standing, retrieval augmented Generation, Legal nlp, argument
Mining, llm’s
I. INTRODUCTION
Lawyers or law practitioners need extensive documentation,
reasoning and proper analysis of case files. Drafting legal
documents requires accuracy, precision and should follow rules
and a format. Argument generation requires logical reasoning.
These processes manually take a lot of t

easoning.
These processes manually take a lot of time for completion.
It has become very easy to automate text generation and
perform efficient document analysis due to the advancements
in artificial intelligence and natural language processing. Large
language models perform the three main functionalities of
this project, which are drafting, argument generation and
summarising. Most tools available online do not focus on an
integrated support system.

lenges.firstly, it is time taking, one well drafted petition can
take up hours and hours to be completed,even when done
by an expert practitioner.secondly,it gives rise to various
different errors.small errors in compli

in between all these Advances, many of the ai based tools
(legal) available today highlight only separated, individual

cal argument generation,analysis of documents and
drafting.
This project proposes an Integrated Law Support System
which provides the following functionalities:
• Legal Drafting: generates structured documents like
petitions, plaints and affidavits based on user inputs.
• Strategic Argument Generation: generating arguments
and predicting the counter arguments of the opponent.

• Chat with PDF and Summarization: solves queries and
summarizes the document.
The system is authenticated through JSON

ocument.
The system is authenticated through JSON Web Tokens
(JWT) and MongoDB is used for storing data. The Gemma
3 (4B) language model is trained with judgements, case files
and other regulatory documents. PDF extraction is done by
PyMuPDF. By integrating these tools, the system provides an
integrated platform for an efficient and productive workflow
for users.

II. LITERATURE SURVEY AND LIMITATIONS
The new age legal systems using AI heavily depend upon
retrieval augmented generation (RAG), which is further inte-
grated with external document retrieval and generative lan-

th external document retrieval and generative lan-
guage models.
A. Retrieval augmented Generation in legal systems
This foundational framework proposed by Patrick Lewis [1]
shows that the combination of dense retrieval and sequence
generation enhances factual grounding in knowledge-intensive
tasks significantly. This methodology supports the chat with
PDF and argument generation modules.
RAG systems which are domain specific are designed
for formal legal applications have played a huge role in

mal legal applications have played a huge role in
extending this framework. Mukund and EshwaraKumar [2] the
dynamic retrieval mechanism which was proposed,optimized
the process of legal summarization by showing improved and
accurate contextual meaning in long and large legal documents
gave higher rouge scores in comparison with an individual
llm summarizer.this work throws light on the importance of
quality of retrieval and chunk granularity in terms of legal
text processing LawPAL [3] applied R

erms of legal
text processing LawPAL [3] applied RAG to improve legal
workflow in India by closely aligning with the processes of
drafting notices and petitions. an adaptive legal RAG pipeline
[4] which further enhance the sensitivity of the context using
hybrid retrieval mechanisms, even though there is an increase
in system complexity.
B. transformer Architectures for Legal NLP
The architecture of the transformer introduced by Ashish
Vaswani in Attention is All You Need [6] influences sequence

Attention is All You Need [6] influences sequence
modeling in a dynamic way and remains the backbone of
new age legal NLP systems. Large generative models like the
one described by Tom B. Brown [7] depict few-shot learning
techniques which facilitate legal drafting. GPT-3 [7], a large-
scale autoregressive transformer, demonstrated strong few-shot
learning capabilities applicable to legal drafting; however, its
tendency towards hallucination and lack of retrieval grounding
limit its direct appl

lack of retrieval grounding
limit its direct applicability to high-stakes legal tasks without
augmentation. Ilias Chalkidis introduced Legal-BERT [5],
which improves performance in classification, retrieval and
semantically understanding tasks. this shows an enhanced and
improved performance in the case of legal text classification,
clause identification (contract) and semantic understanding
tasks.its emphasis and focus on classification and not on
generation restricts it’s direct use and appli

on
generation restricts it’s direct use and applicability in tasks
of argument construction and drafting Pretraining models like
RoBERTa [12] enhance contextual meaning by supporting
document summarization and the chat with PDF module.
C. legal document analysis and Summarization
Saravanan [9] used graph-based ranking for extraction,
which showed improvements in summarization. this approach
designs the document in the form of a graph of sentences
consisting of similarity edges,also recognizes t

s
consisting of similarity edges,also recognizes the major central
sentences as candidates of summary. New age RAG systems
push this by integrating retrieval grounding, which is needed
for chronological event sequences in the timeline module.
Attentive neural retrieval systems [8] improve mapping be-
tween the queries and case files semantically, which further
enhances Q&A in the chat with PDF module. the cuad dataset
gives a benchmark which is expert annotated for clause extra
(contract) using

xpert annotated for clause extra
(contract) using a transformer models.this resulted in obtaining
high F1 scores,however remained restricted to tasks related to
contract analysis.
D. argument mining
Lippi and Torroni [10] depict argument mining techniques
that scan through claims, premises and relations in legal
tasks. Rule extraction from legal regulations [11] facilitates
structured legal reasoning by converting text into machine-
interpretable logic. their work represented and established the

logic. their work represented and established the
theoretical basis for a well structured argument generation
using the case matter provided enabling grounding on the basis
of rules of generated arguments.
E. Research Gaps
an analysis of the above survey shows the following critical
gaps that the current work addresses :
• A need for an unified system: Need for unified law
support system. There is no integrated or centralized sys-
tem that combines drafting, interactive document Q&A,
strategic

nes drafting, interactive document Q&A,
strategic argument and counter-argument generation.
• Limited research on drafting of legal notices, affidavits
and plaints.
• Partial evaluation of drafting systems in the case of
contexts which are specific to a particular jurisdiction.
• Event-date based linking remains weak in large case files.
Lack of focus on chronological reasoning.
• Very low or limited focus on paired argument and
counter-argument strategies.
The present advancements in retrieval

strategies.
The present advancements in retrieval augmented genera-
tion, law domain based transformers and argument mining
are all done independently. There is a large gap in building a

centralized, integrated law based support system that combines
drafting, argument generation and summarization.

III. PROPOSED METHODOLOGY
This methodology presents a platform which is unified
by integrating drafting, document understanding and argu-
ment and logical reasoning. It follows a model (frontend-
backend) which is authenticated secur

l (frontend-
backend) which is authenticated securely using JSON Web
Tokens (JWT) and MongoDB which is used for data storage.
The backend of our model manages the works of document
processing, semantic retrieval and large language model gen-
eration with the help of Gemma 3 (4B) model, to ensure
contextual grounding of the resulting outputs.
In case of the legal drafting module, the module collects
cause of action, jurisdiction, factual background and party
details as a form of structured inputs

d and party
details as a form of structured inputs. These structured inputs
are mapped to pre-existing schemas of the documents like
legal notices, drafts, affidavits and petitions/plaints. Use of
template based prompting strategy combined with retrieval
augmentation. Important procedural rules, legal references and
regulatory provision are retrieved using vector embeddings and
added to the prompt, this results in the generation of structured
drafts that follow formatting conventions.
In case of

fts that follow formatting conventions.
In case of the chat with PDF module, use of OCR for
converting scanned document into machine-readable, logical
chunks indexed using embeddings. These embeddings are
stored in a vector database for retrieval. The most likely seg-
ments are retrieved and added into the generation prompt. This
reduces hallucination and ensures answers remain grounded to
the source context.
The strategic argument and counter-argument generation
module builds logical reasoning

gument generation
module builds logical reasoning using claims, supporting
facts, logical justification and legal provisions. The model
used counterfactual strategies to identify opposing claims,
weaknesses and alternative interpretations which simulates
adversarial learning.
A. Sequence Diagram
The general sequence of operations of the suggested frame-
work is shown in Figure 1.
Fig. 1. Sequence diagram of the Proposed integrated law support system.
B. Description
This sequence diagram shows th

tem.
B. Description
This sequence diagram shows the interaction between the
different components of the system, during the process of a
user query using the chat with PDF module. First the user
uploads a case file or a document through the web interface.
The frontend facilitates the transmission of the document
to backend API. Optical character recognition is applied to
convert the scanned document into a machine-readable format.
This extracted text is broken down into logical chunks and are
tra

ext is broken down into logical chunks and are
transformed into vector embeddings. These embeddings are
stored in the vector database and retrieved back on demand
using semantic indexing.
After the document is processed, the user asks a query
related to the uploaded document. The frontend transmits this
query to the backend API. It now performs a similarity search
in the database to retrieve the most relevant logical chunks or
embeddings (top-K). These chunks serve as factual grounding
in the an

These chunks serve as factual grounding
in the answer generation process. The backend combines the
user query with retrieved contextual information to construct
a retrieval augmented prompt. This prompt is passed to the

Gemma 3 (4B) model. The response generated is returned to
the backend, which forwards it to the display of the user.
The main objective is to reduce the hallucinations, ground-
ing responses, improving factual integrity and ensure answers
are traceable to the original document.

re answers
are traceable to the original document.

D. Layer-Wise Description
User Interface: the components of the UI layer are the
drafting module, chat with PDF interface, and argument–
counter argument module. It accepts the user inputs which
are prompts or PDF documents to generate legal drafts,
summarized output and an argument repor

al drafts,
summarized output and an argument report. Sends API request
to backend via proper authentication.
Backend Layer: The key components of this layer are
REST API server, JWT based authentication. This layer per-
forms proper user validation, transfers requests to AI engine.
It provides security between endpoints.
Processing Layer: The components of this layer are a
drafting engine, a chatbot and a summarizer, a strategic
argument generator. These components are used to generate
structure

r. These components are used to generate
structured petitions, affidavits. Summarizes long documents
and analyzes the case files which saves a lot of time for the
users. Supports in reasoning by understanding and analyzing
the case and providing a strategy for arguments generation.
The backend sends the processed input; the Gemma 3 model
produces the output.
Storage Layer: The components of this layer are the Mon-
goDB database. It is used to store user accounts, document
and chat history. Mongo

re user accounts, document
and chat history. MongoDB is used as it provides a flexible
schema, facilitates document storage.
Document Processing Layer: This layer consists of pub-
licly available documents used for training like judgements
and other regulatory documents. The main functionality of this
layer is extracting text from the PDFs uploaded. PyMuPDF
parses the uploaded PDF files by extracting raw texts and
finally prepares a proper input.

input collection (stage 1: the input provided by user fills a
structured form capturing consisting of defendent details and
plaintiff,court and jurisdiction,cause of action,narrative (fac-
tual) relief sought and important ,relevant dates. the validation
of the input is done on the fronten

the validation
of the input is done on the frontend before transfering to the

schema mapping (stage 2: this backend tries to map the
input which are validated to document schema with respect to
the requested type of document. schemas which are supported
are: civil plaint order VII rule 1,cpc 1908,legal notice under the
IT act 2000 and contract act 1872, petition and affidavit.each
and every schema depicts compulsory fields,supplementary
optiona

ma depicts compulsory fields,supplementary
optional clauses,the required structural organization of the
documenT.
prompt construction and generation (stage 3): a prompt
which is template based is dynamically combined consists
of: 1) the schema of the document and specification type, 2)
user input (facts) which are mapped to corresponding clause
positions, 3) statutory references and procedural rules which
are retrieved using vector similarity search using the legal
knowledge base and finally , 4

rch using the legal
knowledge base and finally , 4) formatting directives which
court headings conventions, verification statement format and
structure of prayer clause this clause. this complex prompt is
submitted to gemma 3 (4B) the resultant output is processed
to maintain formatting consistency and provided to the user
in a downloadable and ready to print format.this system
tries to maintain strict factual inclusion using only the user
provided input and statutory references (retrieved) by a

ed input and statutory references (retrieved) by avoiding
hallucination of dates, names and legal citations.
the chat with pdf module performs a full rag pipeline allow-
ing users to query interactively the uploaded legal documentS.
document ingestion: when uploaded the PyMuPDF ex-
tracts text from the uploaded pdfs.tesseract ocr is invoked
for scanned or documents based on images,text extracted is
properly cleaned by removing headers,footers,page numbers

and noise characters,broken down into o

e numbers

and noise characters,broken down into overlapping segments
or chunks of approximately 512 tokens with a 64 token over-
lap,while maintaining the continuity across chunk boundaries
contextually.
embedding and Indexing: each and every segment or
chunk is parsed through the all-minilm-l6-v2 sentence trans-
former model inorder to generate a 384 dimensional dense
vector embedding.the generated embeddings are stored in a
FAISS index which is associated to the document’s unique
identifier,w

s associated to the document’s unique
identifier,which are immediately present for retrieval upon
query submission.
query processing: when the user provides a query in natural
language,it is embedded using the exactly same sentence
transformer model.a similarity search (cosine) collects the
top-k most relevant segments or chunks from the index,by
setting k=5 by default.the retrieved chunks are now concate-
nated with a context block,and now merged with the query
provided by the user to build and

d with the query
provided by the user to build and construct the retrieval
augmented prompt, it is now submitted to the gemma 3 (4B)
model. grounded Generation: the model is now instructed
strictly to answer mandatorily from the retrieved context and
acknowledge any absence of information from the source
document explicitly.the efficient grounding strategy effectively
removes hallucinations in various majority of test cases. this
module performs multi-turn conversation by adding previous
exchang

multi-turn conversation by adding previous
exchanges with respect to the context of the session,allowing
coherent query follow up with the help of conversational
history which is retained.
the strategic argument generation module paves way for
adversarial logical and legal reasoning using a well defined
four stage pipeline
issue identification: if provided a summary of the case or
an uploaded document, the llm uses zero shot classification
for the identification of primary and secondary legal pr

e identification of primary and secondary legal prob-
lems.these issues are classified under recognized legal heads
like breach of contract, statutory violations,tortious liability
and other constitutional challenges.
argument construction: for each and every recognized
or identified challenge, the module generates a structured
argument consisting of: a) the legal principle (applicable)
or relevant statute, b) the factual foundation from the case
context, c) the application of the legal principl

context, c) the application of the legal principle to already
established facts logically and finally, d) the expected outcome
or the legal relief.arguments are ranked based on the estimated
defensibility and showed in descending order of strength.
counter Argument prediction: using adversarial or counter
factual reasoning, the module produces a corresponding
counter argument for each and every primary argument. the
factual gaps,alternate contextual interpretations, restrictions
in the cited pr

tual interpretations, restrictions
in the cited provisions or deficiencies (procedural) are all
identified by counter arguments.this provides the user with a
comprehensive view of both the sides of dispute even before
attending the proceedings (adversarial pairing).
legal rule alignment: each and every argument and counter
argument is mapped with the top most relevant statutory
provisions already established legal principles and sections
of applicable legislation which are retrieved from the kno

cable legislation which are retrieved from the knowl-
edge base using the vector similarity search.this mapping
makesure’s that every output is identified in legal authority
and not relying only on the model’s knowledge (parametric).
the module uses a well established security architecture
based on jwt based authentication (stateless).when the user
registers,the credentials are hashed via bcrypt using a salt
factor which is configurable before storing in mongodb. this
ensures that the passwords

oring in mongodb. this
ensures that the passwords are never persisted. after a success-
ful login, the backend provides a signed jwt token consisting
of the user’s identification and timestamp (expiry).all the
following api requests must and sure include this token in the
http authentication header,then the backend whose middleware
verifies the signature and expiry even before processing any
request.the role based access control is applied on the api
layer differentiating and separating between

api
layer differentiating and separating between standard users
and admin accounts.the limiting rate is implemented per user
inorder to prevent abuse of the endpoints.mongodb is used to
store all the content which is user generated that include draft
records, history of chat session and metadata of the document
with indexes on user identification and timestamps in separate
collections for accurate and efficient retrieval.each and every
draft records and captures the complete input parameters,
t

ords and captures the complete input parameters,
the resultant output,schema of the document and timestamp
of generation allowing complete traceability of audit for all
outputs of the systems.
F. Enhancements and Innovations
The framework introduces several enhancements:
• It provides a centralized law based support system which
provides multiple functionalities, which include draft gen-
eration, chat with PDF and strategic argument generation.
• Provides real time court room logic by generating

Provides real time court room logic by generating a
proper reasoning for both sides.
• It grounds responses based on the public databases not
merely depending on the LLM’s outputs.
• Use of lightweight models like Gemma 3 (4B) which
reduce the computational cost by maintaining efficient
performance.
• audit Traceability: the complete logging of the inputs,
the retrieved content and context and outputs generated
in mongodb ensures the traceability of each and every
system output.

C. vector database configuration
the vector database is used using FAISS (facebook AI
similarity search) providing highly efficient local deploy-
ment.sentence embeddings produced with the help of all-
minilm-l6-v2 model from the library

help of all-
minilm-l6-v2 model from the library of sentence transformers
resulting in the production of 384 dimensional dense vectors
which balance the quality of representation with computational
efficiency.
the FAISS index type INDEXFLATL2 is used for nearest
neighbour search in a moderate sized document collections.
the system supports an upgrade to INDEXIVFFLAT for larger
sized document collections.this reduces the time of search at
the cost of approximately retrieval.the K value is set to

t of approximately retrieval.the K value is set to 5 (top-
K) retrieval, by default. FAISS indexes are arranged in serial
wise to disk binary files keyed by identifier of the document
and on demand loading into memory.
V. RESULTS AND DISCUSSION
The proposed law support system was tested and evalu-
ated upon three modules: drafting, document analysis and
summarization, and strategic argument and counter-argument
generation. The evaluation emphasized structural consistency,
contextual grounding, l

ed structural consistency,
contextual grounding, logical relevance and practicality. the
testing has been done over 20 legal cases,most commonly on
breach of contract disputes,property disputes and employment
disputes.
A. legal drafting module
The legal drafting module was evaluated by generating a
civil plaint under Order VII Rule 1, Code of Civil Procedure
1908, using the user provided inputs. The resultant draft
comprised of all the required components like identification of
parties, statemen

omponents like identification of
parties, statement of facts, cause of action, jurisdiction clause,
court heading and prayer for relief. The generated document
followed and abided by the conventional format and used the
required legal language. The system significantly maintained
the contextual and factual accuracy like the provided dates,
party names and dispute, and not adding any fabricated data.
The use of aligned regulatory provisions on the basis of case
type for effective grounding. over

basis of case
type for effective grounding. over the 20 test drafting cases,
the module was able to achieve a structural completeness
score of 85.0 percent , the factual accuracy score gave 81.0
percent and the compliance score 85.5 percent as shown in
table IV. small and minor restrictions were observed which
include occasional overformality in the case of simple disputes
(factual narrative section).lack of case specific citations in the
produced drafts.these limitations are described in the f

ed drafts.these limitations are described in the future
work section.
B. chat with pdf module
The case discovery or the chat with PDF module was
evaluated using a legal notice PDF document which was
uploaded. The system was able to process and generate a
relevant summary by capturing contextual and factual elements
successfully. The summary consisted of relevant statutory
references, captured the nature of the dispute i.e. breach of

C. strategy builder module
The strategy builder was evaluated on the basis of accurate,
relevant and meaningful arguments a

s of accurate,
relevant and meaningful arguments and counter-arguments.
In the breach of contract scenario, it generated arguments
for both claimant and defendant sides, simulating adversarial
reasoning. The arguments followed a step by step logical
progression by beginning with identification of legal princi-
ple, then towards the application of facts and ending with
a reasoned claim. Counter-argument depicted alternative in-
terpretations like failure to meet contractual specifications.

judicial precedent citation integration: the existing sys-

in retrieving case specific precedents.the further iterations will
combine an indexed database comprising of court judgements
of the supreme court of India and High courts in India. multi
jurisdiction support: the present working system is curated
for Indian l

of civil procedure legal systems.
domain specific finetuning: Gemma 3 (4B) performs suf-
ficiently on legal tasks using prompt engineering and RAG’s,
supreme court judgements,high court orders,legal texts and
notices n

ements,high court orders,legal texts and

argument consistency.on the basis of lora, parameter efficient
approach of fine tuning will required to minimize operational
cost.
enhanced OCR pipeline: the currently used tesseract based
ocr pipeline works well on 300DPI (high quality scans)
but fails with low resolution or handwritten documents.the
transformer based ocr model will enhan

VII. CONCLUSION
This paper presented the implementation of an integrated
law based support system, which consists of centralized core
functionalities like legal drafting, case discovery and strategy
builder (arguments and counter-arguments generation). This
framework uses retrieval augmented generation (RAG) and
semantic embeddings. The legal drafting module presents the
capability to generate documents adhering to legal, formal con-
ventions. These documents inc

o legal, formal con-
ventions. These documents include civil plaints, notices, drafts,
petitions and affidavits, following and maintaining professional
legal language. The case discovery module processed uploaded
PDFs and generated factually grounded summaries while

maintaining and preserving the chronological references. Strat-
egy builder module simulated adversarial reasoning by pro-
ducing logical arguments and counter-arguments. Integration
of vector based retrieval with large language mod

of vector based retrieval with large language models reduced
hallucinations significantly and enhanced factual grounding.
the qualitative evaluation over 20 legal scenarios showed a
reliable performance. format compliance of 85.0%,context
grounding of 81.0% and argument coherence of 85.5% . the
expert human evaluation gave an overall average of 4.1 out
of the 5.0 over logical soundness,practical usability,structural
compliance and language quality.this confirms that the system
results are pract

ty.this confirms that the system
results are practically valuable and are immediately usable
for working legal documents and strategy tasks. The current
implementation performs well in controlled scenarios; there
is scope for further improvements by using diverse case
datasets and enhanced OCR handling for low quality scanned
documents. judicial precedent citation,enhanced OCR ,multi
jurisdiction support and domain specific fine tuning in the
future directions will further strengthen the system’

ure directions will further strengthen the system’s scope and
practical usability.the proposed system provides a scalable and
efficient tool inorder to support the practitioners in the legal
domain who draft,analyze the documents,and require strategic
argument generation.
